questions.title: GATE | CS | 2017 | Set 2 | Aptitude | Logical | Question 7
questions.question: There are three boxes, one contains apples, another contains oranges and last one contains both apples and oranges. All three are known to be incorrectly labelled. You are permitted to open just one box and then pull out and inspect only one fruit. Which box would you open to determine the contents of all three boxes?
questions.question_html: There are three boxes, one contains apples, another contains oranges and last one contains both apples and oranges. All three are known to be incorrectly labelled. You are permitted to open just one box and then pull out and inspect only one fruit. Which box would you open to determine the contents of all three boxes?

options.text: The box labelled both apples and oranges
options.html: The box labelled both apples and oranges

questions.explanation:
In given question there are three boxes incorrectly labeled:
- Box 3 labeled as Apples and Oranges - Original Content can be Apple or Orangle (This box will have only one fruit, so whichever we pick would  be the correct label). Let's say the picked item was Apple, therefore the box labeled Orange will have Apples and Oranges.)
- Box 2 - Oranges - This box will definitely not have orange (as its mislabeled) and it can not contain only apple (As it is in box 3) - So this will have Original Content- both oranges and Apples
- Box 1 - Apples - This is the leftover box and it can now have Original Content - Orange
Therefore option B is correct
questions.explanation_html:
In given question there are three boxes incorrectly labeled:

  • Box 3 labeled as Apples and Oranges - Original Content can be Apple or Orangle (This box will have only one fruit, so whichever we pick would  be the correct label). Let's say the picked item was Apple, therefore the box labeled Orange will have Apples and Oranges.)
  • Box 2 - Oranges - This box will definitely not have orange (as its mislabeled) and it can not contain only apple (As it is in box 3) - So this will have Original Content- both oranges and Apples
  • Box 1 - Apples - This is the leftover box and it can now have Original Content - Orange

Therefore option B is correct

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2017 | Set 1 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 58
questions.question:
The number of integers between 1 and 500 (both inclusive) that are divisible by 3 or 5 or 7 is ______.
Note: This questions appeared as Numerical Answer Type.
questions.question_html:
The number of integers between 1 and 500 (both inclusive) that are divisible by 3 or 5 or 7 is ______.



Note: This questions appeared as Numerical Answer Type.
questions.explanation:
The general formula for the union of 3 sets is:
(A union B union C) = A + B + C - (A intersect B) - (A intersect C) - (B intersect C) + (A intersect B intersect C).
Assuming,
A = 3, B = 5, C = 7
= 500/3 + 500/5 + 500/7 - 500/3*5 - 500/5*7 - 500/7*3 + 500/105
= 271
Therefore, option C is correct.
Alternate solution
Let a = number divisible by 3
b = number divisible by 5
c = number divisible by 7
n(a) = 166
n(b) = 100
n(c) = 71
n(a∩b) = number divisible by 15 = 33
n(b∩c) = number divisible by 35 = 14
n(a∩c) = number divisible by 21 = 23
n(a∩b∩c) = number divisible by 105 = 4
n(a∪b∪c) = n(a) + n(b) + n(c) - n(a∩b) - n(b∩c) - n(a∩c) + n(a∩b∩c) = 166 + 100 + 71 - 33 - 14 - 23 + 4 = 271
questions.explanation_html:
The general formula for the union of 3 sets is:

(A union B union C) = A + B + C - (A intersect B) - (A intersect C) - (B intersect C) + (A intersect B intersect C).


Assuming,

A = 3, B = 5, C = 7

= 500/3 + 500/5 + 500/7 - 500/3*5 - 500/5*7 - 500/7*3 + 500/105

= 271

Therefore, option C is correct.

Alternate solution

Let a = number divisible by 3

b = number divisible by 5

c = number divisible by 7


n(a) = 166

n(b) = 100

n(c) = 71


n(a∩b) = number divisible by 15 = 33

n(b∩c) = number divisible by 35 = 14

n(a∩c) = number divisible by 21 = 23

n(a∩b∩c) = number divisible by 105 = 4

n(a∪b∪c) = n(a) + n(b) + n(c) - n(a∩b) - n(b∩c) - n(a∩c) + n(a∩b∩c) = 166 + 100 + 71 - 33 - 14 - 23 + 4 = 271

questions.question_html: Arun, Gulab, Neel and Shweta must choose one shirt each from a pile of four shirts coloured red, pink, blue and white respectively. Arun dislikes the colour red and Shweta dislikes the colour white. Gulab and Neel like all the colours. In how many different ways can they choose the shirts so that no one has a shirt with a colour he or she dislikes?
questions.explanation:
Total number of Shirts = 4 , Total candidates =4
- So total combination of possibilities = 4 X 4 = 16
- No.of ways  'Arun chooses red' + No.of ways Shwetha chooses white '=  2
Therefore, Answer is 16-2 = 14.
Alternative approach -
We can solve using inclusion-exclusion: Total possibilities = 4! = 24 Number of ways 'Arun chooses red' or 'Shwetha chooses white' = (Number of ways 'Arun chooses red) + (Number of ways 'Shwetha chooses white) - (Number of ways 'Arun chooses red' and 'Shwetha chooses white) = 6 + 6 − 2 = 10 Required answer = 24 − 10 = 14.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2017 | Set 1 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 8
questions.question: The expression [ (x + y) - |x - y| ] / 2 is equal to
questions.question_html: The expression [ (x + y) - |x - y| ] / 2 is equal to

options.text: the maximum of x and y
options.html: the maximum of x and y

options.text: the minimum of x and y
options.html: the minimum of x and y

questions.explanation:
As we know that, if x > y, then |x - y| = x - y and
if x < y then |x - y| = y - x , because value of |x - y| is always non-negative.
Therefore,
- Case 1: If x > y : (x + y) - |x - y| ] / 2 =  (x + y) - (x - y) ] / 2 = 2y / 2 = y (Minimum of x , y)
- Case 2: If x < y : (x + y) - |x - y| ] / 2 = (x + y) - (y - x) ] / 2 = 2x / 2 = x (Minimum of x , y)
Therefore in both the case we get minimum of (x,y).
So, option B
Note that you can take some random values of x and y, then verify given options.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2017 | Set 1 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 1
questions.question: Find the smallest number y such that y * 162 (y multiplied by 162) is a perfect cube.
questions.question_html: Find the smallest number y such that y * 162 (y multiplied by 162) is a perfect cube.
questions.explanation:
162 = 3 * 3 * 3 * 3 * 2
To make it a perfect cube, we need at least 2 * 2 * 3 * 3 which is 36.
Alternate Solution
Take the prime factor of 162:
162 = 2 * 81 = 2 * 9 * 9 = 2 * 3 * 3 * 3 * 3, now if we add minimum two 2 and two 3, then it will be perfect cube of a number. Therefore, the value of y will be = 2 * 2 * 3 * 3 = 4 * 9 = 36
Note that you can multiple 162 with values in given options, then take cube root with the help of calculator for each option, the answer should be an integer.
questions.explanation_html:

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2019 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 6
questions.question: Ten friends planned to share equally the cost of buying a gifts for their teacher. When two of them decided not to contribute, each of the other friends had to pay Rs. 150 more. The cost of the gift was Rs. _________ .
questions.question_html: Ten friends planned to share equally the cost of buying a gifts for their teacher. When two of them decided not to contribute, each of the other friends had to pay Rs. 150 more. The cost of the gift was Rs. _________ .
questions.explanation:
Let share of each student = x Since cost of gift will be same in both situation, therefore,
→ 10*x = 8*(x + 150)
→ 10*x = 8*x + 8*150
→ 2*x = 8*150
→ x = 8*150 / 2
→ x = 600 Therefore, cost of gift is,
10*x = 10*600 = 6000 Option (C) is correct.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2019 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 5
questions.question:
Two cars at the same time from the same location and go in the same direction. The speed of the first car is 50 km/h and the speed of the second car is 60 km/h. The number of hours it takes for the distance between the two cars to be 20 km is _________.
Note: This was Numerical Type question.
questions.question_html:
Two cars at the same time from the same location and go in the same direction. The speed of the first car is 50 km/h and the speed of the second car is 60 km/h. The number of hours it takes for the distance between the two cars to be 20 km is _________.

Note: This was Numerical Type question.
questions.explanation:
Best approach :
If we consider 1st car stationery, then speed of second car will be 10 Km/h.
so, time required to travel 20 km will be: 2 hours.
Alternative way :
Given two cars at the same time from the same location and go in the same direction with speed of 50 km/h and 60 km/h. Therefore, after 1 hour,
distance traveled by first car (with speed of 50 km/h) = 50*1 = 50 km
And, distance traveled by second car (with speed of 60 km/h) = 60*1 = 60 km
So, distance between these cars = 60 - 50 = 10 km After 2 hour,
distance traveled by first car (with speed of 50 km/h) = 50*2 = 100 km
And, distance traveled by second car (with speed of 60 km/h) = 60*2 = 120 km
So, distance between these cars = 120 - 100 = 20 km Alternative way:
→ (distance traveled by second car) - (distance traveled by first car) = 20 km
→ (S _2 *T) - (S _1 *T) = 20
→ (60*T) - (50*T) = 20
→ 10*T = 20
→ T = 2 Therefore, option (B) is correct.
questions.explanation_html:
Best approach:

If we consider 1st car stationery, then speed of second car will be 10 Km/h.

so, time required to travel 20 km will be: 2 hours.

Alternative way:

Given two cars at the same time from the same location and go in the same direction with speed of 50 km/h and 60 km/h. Therefore, after 1 hour, 

distance traveled by first car (with speed of 50 km/h) = 50*1 = 50 km
And, distance traveled by second car (with speed of 60 km/h) = 60*1 = 60 km

So, distance between these cars = 60 - 50 = 10 km

After 2 hour, 

distance traveled by first car (with speed of 50 km/h) = 50*2 = 100 km
And, distance traveled by second car (with speed of 60 km/h) = 60*2 = 120 km

So, distance between these cars = 120 - 100 = 20 km

Alternative way:

→ (distance traveled by second car) - (distance traveled by first car) = 20 km
→ (S2*T) - (S1*T) = 20 
→ (60*T) - (50*T) = 20 
→ 10*T = 20 
→ T = 2

Therefore, option (B) is correct.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2019 | Aptitude | Logical Aptitude | Question 4
questions.question:
The police arrested four criminals – P, Q, R and S. The criminals knew each other. They made the following statements:
P says “Q committed the crime.”
Q says “S committed the crime.”
R says “ I did not do it.”
S says “What Q said about me is false”. Assume only one of the arrested four committed the crime and only one of the statements made above is true. Who committed the crime?
questions.question_html:
The police arrested four criminals – P, Q, R and S. The criminals knew each other. They made the following statements: 

P says “Q committed the crime.”
Q says “S committed the crime.”
R says “ I did not do it.”
S says “What Q said about me is false”.

Assume only one of the arrested four committed the crime and only one of the statements made above is true. Who committed the crime?
questions.explanation:
Given that only one person committed the crime and only one given statement is correct.
- When P is correct and Q, R, S are false: it implies, Q committed crime,
S did not commit crime,
R committed crime,
S committed crime. This situation is impossible.
- When Q is correct and P, R, S are false: it implies, Q did not commit crime,
S committed crime,
R committed crime,
S committed crime. This situation is impossible.
- When R is correct and P, Q, S are false: it implies, Q did not commit crime,
S did not commit crime,
R did not commit crime,
S committed crime. This situation is impossible.
- When S is correct and P, Q, R are false: it implies, Q did not commit crime,
S did not commit crime,
R committed crime,
S did not commit crime. This situation is possible means R has committed crime.
So, option (B) is correct.
questions.explanation_html:
Given that only one person committed the crime and only one given statement is correct.

  • When P is correct and Q, R, S are false: it implies, 
    Q committed crime,
    S did not commit crime,
    R committed crime,
    S committed crime.
    This situation is impossible.
  • When Q is correct and P, R, S are false: it implies, 
    Q did not commit crime,
    S committed crime,
    R committed crime,
    S committed crime.
    This situation is impossible.
  • When R is correct and P, Q, S are false: it implies, 
    Q did not commit crime,
    S did not commit crime,
    R did not commit crime,
    S committed crime.
    This situation is impossible.
  • When S is correct and P, Q, R are false: it implies, 
    Q did not commit crime,
    S did not commit crime,
    R committed crime,
    S did not commit crime.
    This situation is possible means R has committed crime.

So, option (B) is correct.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2019 | Aptitude | Spatial Aptitude | Question 43
questions.question:
Consider the following sets:
- S _1 : Set of all recursively enumerable languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.
- S _2 : Set of all syntactically valid C programs.
- S _3 : Set of all languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.
- S _4 : Set of all non-regular languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.
Which of the above sets are uncountable?
questions.question_html:
Consider the following sets:

  • S1: Set of all recursively enumerable languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.
  • S2: Set of all syntactically valid C programs.
  • S3: Set of all languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.
  • S4: Set of all non-regular languages over the alphabet {0, 1}.


Which of the above sets are uncountable?
questions.explanation:
- Recursively enumerable languages are countable.
- Syntactically valid C program can be represented with CFG. CFG generates CFL, CFL is countable.
- All languages over {0, 1} may not be countable, because they may also lie in the region of 2 ^Σ* .
- Set of regular languages are countable, non-regular languages may not be countable.
Option (C) is true.
questions.explanation_html:
1. Recursively enumerable languages are countable.
  2. Syntactically valid C program can be represented with CFG. CFG generates CFL, CFL is countable.
  3. All languages over {0, 1} may not be countable, because they may also lie in the region of 2Σ*.
  4. Set of regular languages are countable, non-regular languages may not be countable.

Option (C) is true.

questions.question_html:
The value of 351 mod 5 is _________ .


Note: This was Numerical Type question.
questions.explanation:
According to
chinese remainder theorem :
= 3 ^51 mod 5
= (3 ^3 ) ^17 mod 5
= (27) ^17 mod 5
= {(2 ^2 ) ^8 *2 ^1 } mod 5
= {(4) ^8 *(2)} mod 5
= {(-1) ^8 *(2)} mod 5
= {(1*2)} mod 5
= {(2)} mod 5
= 2 Note:
You can solve it directly by using power and mod operations in given calculator. So, option (B) is correct.
questions.explanation_html:
According to

chinese remainder theorem

: 

= 351 mod 5 
= (33)17 mod 5 
= (27)17 mod 5
= {(22)8*21} mod 5
= {(4)8*(2)} mod 5
= {(-1)8*(2)} mod 5
= {(1*2)} mod 5
= {(2)} mod 5
= 2

Note:

You can solve it directly by using power and mod operations in given calculator. So, option (B) is correct.

questions.question_html:

  • Request by X: Due to pollen allergy, I want to avoid a wing next to the garden.

  • Request by Z: I believe in Vaastu and so I want to stay in South-West wing.

The shaded rooms are already occupied. WR is washroom
questions.explanation: In all other option except D, Z is not staying in south-west wing. Since Z believe in vastu, he can stay in Any of the south-west wings but not in any other wing. In option (D) X is away from the garden as well as Y is away from the washroom. So, option (D) is correct.

questions.question: In a college, there are three student clubs, Sixty students are only in the Drama club, 80 students are only in the Dance club, 30 students are only in Maths club, 40 students are in both Drama and Dance clubs, 12 students are in both Dance and Maths clubs, 7 students are in both Drama and Maths clubs, and 2 students are in all clubs. If 75% of the students in the college are not in any of these clubs, then the total number of students in the college is _____________.
questions.question_html: In a college, there are three student clubs, Sixty students are only in the Drama club, 80 students are only in the Dance club, 30 students are only in Maths club, 40 students are in both Drama and Dance clubs, 12 students are in both Dance and Maths clubs, 7 students are in both Drama and Maths clubs, and 2 students are in all clubs. If 75% of the students in the college are not in any of these clubs, then the total number of students in the college is _____________.
questions.explanation:
- Note that given 2 students are in all clubs so these are common in given each two group.
- After deducting 2: Venn Diagram
Therefore total number of students participating in any of these clubs are:
= 60 + 80 + 30 + 38 + 2 + 10 + 5
= 225 Given only these are 25% of the total number of students in the college (75% of the students in the college are not in any of these clubs.). Therefore total total number of students in the college:
x*0.25 = 225
= 225/0.25
= 900 So, option (C) is correct.

questions.question: Which one of the options best describes the transformation of the 2-dimensional figure P to Q , and then to R , as shown? [GATE 2023 || 2 Marks || MCQ]

options.text:
Operation 1 : A clockwise rotation by 90º about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure
Operation 2 : A reflection along a horizontal line

Operation 1: A clockwise rotation by 90º about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure

Operation 2: A reflection along a horizontal line

questions.explanation:
To determine the transformation of the 2-dimensional figure P to Q and
then to R, let's analyze the changes in the figure step by step:
From P to Q:
The figure is rotated clockwise by 90º about an axis perpendicular to the
plane of the figure. The triangular portion with the dot moves to the right.
Based on the change, option (A) "Operation 1: A clockwise rotation by 90º
about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure" seems correct.
From Q to R:
The figure is reflected along a horizontal line. The triangular portion with
the dot now appears below the line.
Based on the change, option (A) "Operation 2: A reflection along a
horizontal line" seems correct.
Therefore, the correct answer is (A)

questions.title: Aptitude | Verbal Aptitude | Question 7 | CS | 2023
questions.question:
The country of Zombieland is in distress since more than 75% of its working population is suffering from serious health issues. Studies conducted by competent health experts concluded that a complete lack of physical exercise among its working population was one of the leading causes of their health issues. As one of the measures to address the problem, the Government of Zombieland has decided to provide monetary incentives to those who ride bicycles to work.
Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following statements can be logically inferred with certainty ? [GATE 2023 || 2 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html:
The country of Zombieland is in distress since more than 75% of its working population is suffering from serious health issues. Studies conducted by competent health experts concluded that a complete lack of physical exercise among its working population was one of the leading causes of their health issues. As one of the measures to address the problem, the Government of Zombieland has decided to provide monetary incentives to those who ride bicycles to work.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following statements can be logically inferred with certainty? [GATE 2023 || 2 Marks || MCQ]

options.text: All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work.
options.html: All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work.

options.text: Riding bicycles will ensure that all of the working population of Zombieland is free of health issues.
options.html: Riding bicycles will ensure that all of the working population of Zombieland is free of health issues.

options.text: The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding bicycles as mandatory.
options.html: The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding bicycles as mandatory.

options.text: The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycles is a form of physical exercise.
options.html: The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycles is a form of physical exercise.

questions.explanation:
The statement "All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work" is illogical, because the information provided does not imply that everyone will begin riding bicycles to work.
The statement "Riding bicycles will ensure that all of Zombieland's working population is free of health issues" cannot be logically inferred because other health issues may be affecting the population.
The statement "The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding bicycles as mandatory" cannot be logically inferred because no information on what the health experts recommended to the government is provided.
Bicycling is considered a form of physical exercise by the Zombieland government. It can be logically inferred with certainty.
questions.explanation_html:
The statement "All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work" is illogical, because the information provided does not imply that everyone will begin riding bicycles to work.
The statement "Riding bicycles will ensure that all of Zombieland's working population is free of health issues" cannot be logically inferred because other health issues may be affecting the population.
The statement "The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding bicycles as mandatory" cannot be logically inferred because no information on what the health experts recommended to the government is provided.

Bicycling is considered a form of physical exercise by the Zombieland government. It can be logically inferred with certainty.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 4 | CS | 2023
questions.question: A survey for a certain year found that 90% of pregnant women received medical care at least once before giving birth. Of these women, 60% received medical care from doctors, while 40% received medical care from other healthcare providers. Given this information, which one of the following statements can be inferred with certainty?  [GATE 2023 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: A survey for a certain year found that 90% of pregnant women received medical care at least once before giving birth. Of these women, 60% received medical care from doctors, while 40% received medical care from other healthcare providers. Given this information, which one of the following statements can be inferred with certainty? [GATE 2023 || 1 Marks || MCQ]

options.text: More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
options.html: More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.

options.text: Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
options.html: Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.

options.text: More than half of pregnant women received medical care at most once from a doctor.
options.html: More than half of pregnant women received medical care at most once from a doctor.

options.text: Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at most once from a doctor.
options.html: Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at most once from a doctor.

questions.explanation:
Total women = 100
Pregnant women received medical care at least.
Once before giving birth = 90% of 100 = 90
Out of 90 women 60% received medical care from doctors = 60% of 90 = 54
While 40% received from other health care providers = 40% of 90 = 46
Here, more than half of pregnant women (54) received medical care at least once from a doctor.
questions.explanation_html:
Total women = 100 
Pregnant women received medical care at least. 
Once before giving birth = 90% of 100 = 90 
Out of 90 women 60% received medical care from doctors = 60% of 90 = 54 

While 40% received from other health care providers = 40% of 90 = 46 
Here, more than half of pregnant women (54) received medical care at least once from a doctor.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 3 | CS | 2023
questions.question:
A series of natural numbers F1,F2,F3,F4,F5,F6,F7 obeys Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1 for all integers n>=2.
If F6=37 and F7=60 Then what is the value of F1? [GATE 2023 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html:
A series of natural numbers F1,F2,F3,F4,F5,F6,F7 obeys Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1 for all integers n>=2.

If F6=37 and F7=60 Then what is the value of F1? [GATE 2023 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
According to the given data we have to find the value of F1 using the recurrence relation
Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1
While putting the value of F6 and F7 we will get the value of F5 which is 23
Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1 so after this we will look for F4 which will 14
And by following F4 and F5 we will calculate the value of F3 which is 9
and so on value of F1 is 4 .
questions.explanation_html:
According to the given data we have to find the value of F1 using the recurrence relation

Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1

While putting the value of F6 and F7 we will get the value of F5 which is 23

Fn+1=Fn+Fn-1 so after this we will look for F4 which will 14

And by following F4 and F5 we will calculate the value of F3 which is 9

and so on value of F1 is 4 .

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 4 | CS | 2024 | Set 1
questions.question: The number of coins of ₹1, ₹5, and ₹10 denominations that a person has are in the ratio 5:3:13. Of the total amount, the percentage of money in ₹5 coins is [GATE 2024 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: The number of coins of ₹1, ₹5, and ₹10 denominations that a person has are in the ratio 5:3:13. Of the total amount, the percentage of money in ₹5 coins is [GATE 2024 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
Given the ratio of coins: ₹1 : ₹5 : ₹10 = 5x : 3x : 13x
Total number of coins = 5x + 3x + 13x = 21x
Total amount of money = ₹(5x + 15x + 130x) = ₹150x
Percentage of money in ₹5 coins = (Amount contributed by ₹5 coins / Total amount) * 100%
= (15x / 150x) * 100%
= 10%
questions.explanation_html:
Given the ratio of coins: ₹1 : ₹5 : ₹10 = 5x : 3x : 13x

Total number of coins = 5x + 3x + 13x = 21x

Total amount of money = ₹(5x + 15x + 130x) = ₹150x

Percentage of money in ₹5 coins = (Amount contributed by ₹5 coins / Total amount) * 100%
= (15x / 150x) * 100%
= 10%

questions.explanation_html:

Considering year 2007, Electricity generated through renewable sources = Hydro + Solar + Wind = 30% + 5% + 5% = 40 %

Similarly, if we consider for the year 2023, Electricity generated through renewable sources = 35% + 20% +  10% = 65%.

Therefore, Percentage increase in the electricity generation = (65-40)/40 % = 62.5% 
Hence, the correct option is D.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 4 | CS | 2024 | Set 2
questions.question:
For positive non-zero real variables x and y, if
ln((x+y)/2) = 1/2[ln(x) + ln(y)]
then, the value of x/y + y/x
[GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html:
For positive non-zero real variables x and y, if

ln((x+y)/2) = 1/2[ln(x) + ln(y)] 

then, the value of x/y + y/x

[GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
As given in the question,
ln((x+y)/2) = 1/2[ ln x + ln y]
ln((x+y)/2) = 1/2 [ ln xy ]
ln((x+y)/2) = ln√(xy)
Removing ln from both sides, we get
(x+y)/2 = √(xy)
x2 + y2 + 2xy = 4xy
x/y + y/x + 2 = 4
x/y + y/x = 2
Hence, the correct option is C.
questions.explanation_html:

x2 + y2 + 2xy = 4xy
x/y + y/x + 2 = 4
x/y + y/x = 2
Hence, the correct option is C.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 3 | CS | 2024 | Set 2
questions.question: In an engineering college of 10,000 students, 1,500 like neither their core branches nor other branches. The number of students who like their core branches is 1/4th of the number of students who like other branches. The number of students who like both their core and other branches is 500. The number of students who like their core branches is [GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: In an engineering college of 10,000 students, 1,500 like neither their core branches nor other branches. The number of students who like their core branches is 1/4th of the number of students who like other branches. The number of students who like both their core and other branches is 500. The number of students who like their core branches is [GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
From the given data, the Total number of students = 10000.
Number of students who are not interested in both = 1500.
Remaining Students = 10000-1500 = 8500.
Let x represent core students, therefore, 4x will represent not core students.
=> x+4x = 8500+500.
5x = 9000.
X = 1800.
Hence, number of core students is 1800.
Hence, the correct option is A.

X = 1800.

Hence, number of core students is 1800.
Hence, the correct option is A.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 2 | CS | 2024 | Set 2
questions.question: Two wizards try to create a spell using all the four elements, water, air, fire, and earth. For this, they decide to mix all these elements in all possible orders. They also decide to work independently. After trying all possible combination of elements, they conclude that the spell does not work. How many attempts does each wizard make before coming to this conclusion, independently? [GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: Two wizards try to create a spell using all the four elements, water, air, fire, and earth. For this, they decide to mix all these elements in all possible orders. They also decide to work independently. After trying all possible combination of elements, they conclude that the spell does not work. How many attempts does each wizard make before coming to this conclusion, independently? [GATE 2024 || Set-2 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
As per the given data in the question, there are four elements. Hence, we will use a combination of four elements.
Therefore, the result is 4 * 3 * 2 * 1 = 24.
Hence, the correct option is A.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2025 | Set 2 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 3
questions.question: If 𝑃𝑒 ^𝑥 = 𝑄𝑒 ^−𝑥 for all real values of 𝑥, which one of the following statements is true?
questions.question_html: If 𝑃𝑒 𝑥 = 𝑄𝑒 −𝑥 for all real values of 𝑥, which one of the following statements is true?
questions.explanation:
The given equation is: 𝑃𝑒 ^𝑥 = 𝑄𝑒 ^−𝑥
=> 𝑃𝑒 ^2𝑥 = 𝑄
For this to be true for all x, Pe ^2x must always be equal to a constant Q. This is only possible if both sides are constant. The only way this holds is if both P=0 and Q=0, so that both sides remain 0.
Hence, the correct answer is P=Q=0
questions.explanation_html:
The given equation is: 𝑃𝑒 𝑥 = 𝑄𝑒 −𝑥

=> 𝑃𝑒 2𝑥 = 𝑄
For this to be true for all x, Pe2x must always be equal to a constant Q. This is only possible if both sides are constant. The only way this holds is if both P=0 and Q=0, so that both sides remain 0.

Hence, the correct answer is P=Q=0

questions.question: Let 𝑝1 and 𝑝2 denote two arbitrary prime numbers. Which one of the following statements is correct for all values of 𝑝1 and 𝑝2?

options.text: 𝑝1 + 𝑝2 is not a prime number
options.html: 𝑝1 + 𝑝2 is not a prime number

questions.explanation:
Let's consider p1​=2 and p2​=3 as two prime numbers
p1+p2=5 is a prime number. Hence, Option (A) is incorrect.
p1p2=6 is not a prime number. Hence, Option (B) is correct.
p1+p2+1=6 is not a prime number. Hence, Option (C) is incorrect.
p1p2+1=7 is a prime number in this case but it not valid for all prime numbers. Take, p1=5 and p2=7 then, p1p2+1=36 which is not prime. Hence, Option (D) is incorrect.

questions.title: Aptitude | Verbal Aptitude | Question 1 | CS | 2025 | Set 1
questions.question:
Ravi had ______ younger brother who taught at ______ university. He was widely regarded as ______ honorable man. Select the option with the correct sequence of articles to fill in the blanks.
[GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html:
Ravi had ______ younger brother who taught at ______ university. He was widely regarded as ______ honorable man. Select the option with the correct sequence of articles to fill in the blanks.

[GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
The word younger is a comparative adjective, and we generally use "a" before singular, non-specific countable nouns.
"university" → The word "university" begins with the letter 'u', which is a vowel, but it has a "yu" (consonant sound) as in you-niversity.
"honorable man" → The word "honorable" begins with 'h', but the 'h' is silent. It is pronounced "onorable", which starts with a vowel sound.
We use "an" before words that start with a vowel sound.
questions.explanation_html:
The word younger is a comparative adjective, and we generally use "a" before singular, non-specific countable nouns.

"university" → The word "university" begins with the letter 'u', which is a vowel, but it has a "yu" (consonant sound) as in you-niversity.

"honorable man" → The word "honorable" begins with 'h', but the 'h' is silent. It is pronounced "onorable", which starts with a vowel sound.

We use "an" before words that start with a vowel sound.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2025 | Set 2 | Aptitude | Logical Aptitude | Question 7
questions.question: If IMAGE and FIELD are coded as FHBNJ and EMFJG respectively then, which one among the given options is the most appropriate code for BEACH ?
questions.question_html: If IMAGE and FIELD are coded as FHBNJ and EMFJG respectively then, which one among the given options is the most appropriate code for BEACH ?
questions.explanation:
For IMAGE :
- I → J (next letter of I)
- M → N (next letter of M)
- A → B (next letter of A)
- G → H (next letter of G)
- E → F (next letter of E)
Then, the letters are placed in reverse order: FHBNJ
For FIELD :
- F → G
- I → J
- E → F
- L → M
- D → E
Then, the letters are placed in reverse order: EMFJG
Now, for beach
- B → C
- E → F
- A → B
- C → D
- H → I
Then, placing the letters in reverse order: IDBFC
questions.explanation_html:
For IMAGE:

  • I → J (next letter of I)
  • M → N (next letter of M)
  • A → B (next letter of A)
  • G → H (next letter of G)
  • E → F (next letter of E)

Then, the letters are placed in reverse order: FHBNJ

For FIELD:

  • F → G
  • I → J
  • E → F
  • L → M
  • D → E

Then, the letters are placed in reverse order: EMFJG

Now, for beach

  • B → C
  • E → F
  • A → B
  • C → D
  • H → I

Then, placing the letters in reverse order: IDBFC

questions.title: Aptitude | Verbal Aptitude | Question 2 | CS | 2025 | Set 1
questions.question:
The CEO’s decision to downsize the workforce was considered myopic because it sacrificed long-term stability to accommodate short-term gains. Select the most appropriate option that can replace the word “myopic” without changing the meaning of the sentence.
[GATE 2025 || DA || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html:
The CEO’s decision to downsize the workforce was considered myopic because it sacrificed long-term stability to accommodate short-term gains. Select the most appropriate option that can replace the word “myopic” without changing the meaning of the sentence.

[GATE 2025 || DA || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
"Myopic" literally means nearsighted (unable to see far).
Figuratively, it means shortsighted, lacking foresight, or focusing only on immediate results rather than considering long-term consequences.
(A) Visionary
Incorrect. Opposite of myopic. A visionary person has long-term foresight.
(B) Shortsighted
Correct. It directly means lacking long-term perspective, which fits the meaning of "myopic."
(C) Progressive
Incorrect. Progressive means forward-thinking and future-oriented, which is the opposite of myopic.
(D) Innovative
Incorrect. Innovative means creative and introducing new ideas, which does not match the meaning of myopic.
questions.explanation_html:
"Myopic" literally means nearsighted (unable to see far).

Figuratively, it means shortsighted, lacking foresight, or focusing only on immediate results rather than considering long-term consequences.

(A) Visionary

Incorrect. Opposite of myopic. A visionary person has long-term foresight.

(B) Shortsighted

Correct. It directly means lacking long-term perspective, which fits the meaning of "myopic."

(C) Progressive

Incorrect. Progressive means forward-thinking and future-oriented, which is the opposite of myopic.

(D) Innovative

Incorrect. Innovative means creative and introducing new ideas, which does not match the meaning of myopic.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantative Aptitude | Question 3 | CS | 2025 | Set 1
questions.question: The average marks obtained by a class in an examination were calculated as 30.8. However, while checking the marks entered, the teacher found that the marks of one student were entered incorrectly as 24 instead of 42. After correcting the marks, the average becomes 31.4. How many students does the class have? [GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: The average marks obtained by a class in an examination were calculated as 30.8. However, while checking the marks entered, the teacher found that the marks of one student were entered incorrectly as 24 instead of 42. After correcting the marks, the average becomes 31.4. How many students does the class have? [GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 1 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
The difference in average is 31.4 − 30.8 = 0.6
The total correction was 42 − 24 = 18
So, number of students = 18 ÷ 0.6 = 30
Answer: C) 30
questions.explanation_html:
The difference in average is 31.4 − 30.8 = 0.6
The total correction was 42 − 24 = 18
So, number of students = 18 ÷ 0.6 = 30

Answer: C) 30

Once I planned to write a book of poems entirely about the things in my pocket. But I found it would be too long: and the age of the great epics is past.”

questions.explanation_html:
From the given values:

  • X(0)=20
  • X(1)=X(0)+I(1)=20+(−4)=16
  • X(2)=X(1)+I(2)=16+10=26
  • X(3)=X(2)+I(3)=26+15=41

This follows:

X(i)=X(i−1)+I(i)

Now, let's verify Y(i):

From the given values:

  • Y(0)=20
  • Y(1)=Y(0)×I(1)=20×(−4)=−80
  • Y(2)=Y(1)×I(2)=−80×10=−800
  • Y(3)=Y(2)×I(3)=−800×15=−12000

This follows:

Y(i)=Y(i−1)×I(i)

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2025 | Set 2 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 9
questions.question:
In the given figure, PQRS is a square of side 2 cm and PLMN is a rectangle. The corner L of the rectangle is on the side QR. Side MN of the rectangle passes through the corner S of the square. What is the area (in cm2 ) of the rectangle PLMN? Note: The figure shown is representative.
figure
questions.question_html: In the given figure, PQRS is a square of side 2 cm and PLMN is a rectangle. The corner L of the rectangle is on the side QR. Side MN of the rectangle passes through the corner S of the square. What is the area (in cm2 ) of the rectangle PLMN? Note: The figure shown is representative.
questions.explanation:
PQRS is a square with side 2 cm.
PLMN is a rectangle, tilted inside the square.
MN passes through S, meaning the rectangle is diagonally placed.
Using geometry, the length and width of PLMN are both √2 cm.
Area of PLMN = length × width = (√2 × √2) = 2√2 cm².

questions.explanation:

Option 3 (Bridges on Q, R, T, and V) and Option 4 (Bridges on P, Q, S, U, and V) ensures all zones are connected but option 3 contains least number of bridges.
Hence, option 3 is correct.

Hence, option 3 is correct.

questions.title: Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 10 | CS | 2025 | Set 1
questions.question: A shop has 4 distinct flavors of ice-cream. One can purchase any number of scoops of any flavor. The order in which the scoops are purchased is inconsequential. If one wants to purchase 3 scoops of ice-cream, in how many ways can one make that purchase? [GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 2 Marks || MCQ]
questions.question_html: A shop has 4 distinct flavors of ice-cream. One can purchase any number of scoops of any flavor. The order in which the scoops are purchased is inconsequential. If one wants to purchase 3 scoops of ice-cream, in how many ways can one make that purchase? [GATE 2025 || Set-1 || 2 Marks || MCQ]
questions.explanation:
We need to distribute 3 scoops among 4 flavors ⇒ combinations with repetition .
Formula:
Number of ways= [Tex]\text{(}\frac{n+r-1}{r}\text{)}[/Tex]
where
- n = 4 (flavors)
- r = 3 (scoops)
So,
[Tex]\text{(}\frac{4+3-1}{3}\text{)}[/Tex] = [Tex]\text{(}\frac{6}{3}\text{)}[/Tex] = [Tex]\text{(}\frac{6*5*4}{3*2*1}\text{)}[/Tex] =20
Answer: B) 20 .
questions.explanation_html:
We need to distribute 3 scoops among 4 flavors ⇒ combinations with repetition.

Formula:

Number of ways=[Tex]\text{(}\frac{n+r-1}{r}\text{)}[/Tex]

where

  • n = 4 (flavors)
  • r = 3 (scoops)

So,

[Tex]\text{(}\frac{4+3-1}{3}\text{)}[/Tex]=[Tex]\text{(}\frac{6}{3}\text{)}[/Tex]=[Tex]\text{(}\frac{6*5*4}{3*2*1}\text{)}[/Tex]=20

Answer: B) 20.

questions.title: GATE | CS | 2014 | Set 1 | Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude | Question 64
questions.question: A pennant is a sequence of numbers, each number being 1 or 2. An n-pennant is a sequence of numbers with sum equal to n. For example, (1,1,2) is a 4-pennant. The set of all possible 1-pennants is {(1)}, the set of all possible 2-pennants is {(2), (1,1)} and the set of all 3-pennants is {(2,1), (1,1,1), (1,2)}. Note that the pennant (1,2) is not the same as the pennant (2,1). The number of 10- pennants is ______________.
questions.question_html: A pennant is a sequence of numbers, each number being 1 or 2. An n-pennant is a sequence of numbers with sum equal to n. For example, (1,1,2) is a 4-pennant. The set of all possible 1-pennants is {(1)}, the set of all possible 2-pennants is {(2), (1,1)} and the set of all 3-pennants is {(2,1), (1,1,1), (1,2)}. Note that the pennant (1,2) is not the same as the pennant (2,1). The number of 10- pennants is ______________.
questions.explanation_html:
1-pennant {(1)} - #12-pennant {(1,1),(2)} - #23-pennant {(1,1,1),(1,2),(2,1)} - #34-pennant {(1,1,1,1),(2,2),(1,1,2),(1,2,1),(2,1,1)} - #55-pennant {(1,1,1,1,1),(2,1,1,1),(1,2,1,1),(1,1,2,1),            (1,1,1,2),(2,2,1),(2,1,2),(1,2,2)} - #8

If one observe carefully they are the terms(part of) a Fibonacci series. (0,1,1,2,3,5,8,13 ....). Hence the # of 10-pennant is the 12th term of the series ie. 89
